question.text: Arbitration initiated after six months by a Market Participant will require payment of additional fee of:

answer.text: 50% of the applicable fees per additional month of delay, on non-refundable basis

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Late arbitration initiation (after 6 months) by a Market Participant attracts an additional 50% of fees per additional month of delay. **The Trap:** This only applies to Market Participants, not investors. For initiation between 1-6 months, fees are doubled. **Cross-Exam Context:** Progressive late fees discourage delays in dispute resolution. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The increasing fee structure creates strong incentive for timely arbitration initiation.
